title: West Covina Criminal Defense Attorney - Farris Law Firm
metaDescription: Facing criminal charges in West Covina? Farris Law Firm offers personalized, strategic defense to protect your rights. Call for a free consultation.
contentHtml:
The moment you’re accused of a crime in West Covina, your life shifts instantly. Court dates replace your daily routines, and the future is scary and uncertain. Maybe it was a misunderstanding, a mistake, or a moment taken out of context. Or perhaps you just made a big mistake.

West Covina Criminal Defense Lawyer at Farris Law Firm, believe no one should be defined by a single accusation. We approach every case with the care and focus it deserves, crafting defense strategies that reflect your circumstances.

The goal isn’t just to address the charges—it’s to allow you to move forward on your terms.

West Covina Criminal Defense Guide

  • Why Choose Farris Law Firm?
  • How Much Is My Case Worth?
  • How Farris Law Firm Maximizes Your Compensation
  • West Covina’s Criminal Landscape
  • Statute of Limitations

Why Choose Farris Law Firm?

Choosing the right criminal defense lawyer can shape the outcome of your case and your future. Farris Law Firm prioritizes your understanding, dignity, and rights throughout the legal process.

Here’s what sets us apart:

  • A focus on education, not fear
    We explain your options clearly and provide realistic guidance without resorting to scare tactics or high-pressure sales.
  • Your story matters
    We don’t rely solely on law enforcement’s account. Listening to your side of the story is at the core of building a strong defense.
  • Prepared to fight for you
    Whether negotiating a plea deal or presenting your case in court, we’re committed to securing the best possible outcome for you.
  • Dedicated and personalized attention
    Keeping a limited client list ensures that every case receives our full focus and the highest-quality legal defense.
  • Local knowledge and trusted relationships
    Based in downtown Burbank, steps from the courthouse, we’ve cultivated strong relationships with judges, prosecutors, and court staff, allowing us to approach cases with insight and care.

Meet the Farris Firm

Charles and Beatrice Farris established Farris Law Firm in 2024 to bring a personalized approach to criminal defense in the Valley.

With years of experience handling thousands of criminal cases, they built the firm to serve the community with honesty, transparency, and exceptional legal representation.

Farris Law Firm operates differently from high-volume firms, focusing solely on local cases to provide the attention and knowledge each client deserves.

Whether it’s defending against DUI charges, expunging a record, or facing theft accusations, our team guides you through the criminal justice system with professionalism and care.

How Much Is My Case Worth?

The value of your case depends on several key factors, each playing a role in determining the compensation you may receive.

Factors affecting compensation:

  • The severity of your injuries: More serious injuries typically lead to higher compensation.
  • Medical expenses: Past and future medical bills, rehabilitation costs, and therapy can significantly impact your claim.
  • Lost income: Time missed from work, reduced earning potential, or permanent disability may increase the settlement amount.
  • Property damage: Repairs or replacement costs for damaged property, like a vehicle, are considered.
  • Pain and suffering: Emotional distress and the physical toll of your injuries factor into non-economic damages.

Types of compensation

Victims can pursue several types of damages, including:

  • Economic damages: These include tangible costs like medical bills, lost wages, and property damage.
  • Non-economic damages: Pain, suffering, and emotional distress fall into this category.
  • Punitive damages: In rare cases, courts may award punitive damages as a penalty if the at-fault party acted with gross negligence.

How Farris Law Firm Maximizes Your Compensation

Insurance companies aim to minimize payouts, but we know their tactics and use proven strategies to counter them. Here’s how we help:

  • Detailed case evaluation: To calculate an accurate settlement value, we assess every factor affecting your claim.
  • Negotiation experience: With years of experience, we resist lowball offers to secure the amount you deserve.
  • Trial readiness: If negotiations fail, we’re fully prepared to take your case to court and advocate for your rights before a judge or jury.
  • Local insights: Our firm’s strong relationships with local court staff and familiarity with area judges give us a unique advantage in building and presenting your case.

We take the burden off your shoulders and hold the insurance companies accountable so you can focus on healing and moving forward.

West Covina’s Criminal Landscape

West Covina has a crime rate of approximately 27.38 incidents per 1,000 residents, which is higher than 76% of California cities and towns.

Violent crimes, including assault and robbery, occur at a rate of about 4.09 per 1,000 residents. In contrast, property crimes, such as burglary and motor vehicle theft, are more prevalent, at 23.29 per 1,000 residents.

Certain areas in West Covina have higher crime rates. The west neighborhoods report a higher crime rate, with a chance of 1 in 16 of becoming a victim, compared to the north part of the city, where the chance is 1 in 44.

Unique local factors

  • Commuter Traffic: West Covina’s proximity to major highways like I-10 leads to significant commuter traffic, increasing the likelihood of traffic-related offenses and accidents.
  • Retail Hubs: Areas with high retail activity, such as shopping centers, tend to experience higher rates of property crimes, including theft and vandalism.
  • Residential Density: Densely populated residential zones may see elevated instances of domestic disputes and related offenses.

Statute of Limitations

In California, the statute of limitations varies by offense:

  • Misdemeanors: Generally, prosecution must commence within one year of the offense.
  • Felonies: The timeframe varies; for example, prosecution for certain felonies must begin within three years, while more severe crimes may have extended periods or no limitation.

Engaging an attorney as soon as possible ensures that your defense is initiated within these critical timeframes, preserving your legal rights.

Take control of your future today. Criminal charges don’t have to define your life, but acting quickly is key. At Farris Law Firm, we provide personalized, strategic defense designed to protect your rights and achieve the best possible outcome for your case.

Whether you’re facing accusations of theft, vandalism, or a DUI, our local focus and commitment to your story set us apart. Don’t wait for the system to decide your fate—put an experienced legal advocate on your side.

Call us now at (818) 861-0861 for a confidential consultation and start building the defense you deserve.
